rulings.Case Title: PILAR GRAHAM VS. CHARLES SCHWAB & CO., INC A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 28, 2011, line 10. PLAINTIFF PILAR GRAHAM'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION on the cause of action for Failure to Accomodate is denied. Defendant submits declarations who directly contradict plaintiff's testimony of what she told them about her alleged disabilities. Moreover, motion for summary adjudication cannot be granted on the liability issue alone. Plaintiff failed to establish the amount of her damages. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: PILAR GRAHAM VS. CHARLES SCHWAB & CO., INC A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Charles & Schwab & Co., Inc'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 28, 2011, line 10. Defendant Charles & Schwab & Co., Inc's Motion For Summary Judgment is granted. An essential element of both the cause of action for failure to provide a reasonable accomodation and a discrimination cause of action is that defendant knew plaintiff had disability as defined under FEHA. Defendant has satisfied its burden of demonstrating that no triable issue of fact exists as to whether plaintiff informed defendant of a disability as understood by FEHA. Pensinger v. Bowsmith (1998) 60 C.A. 4th 709. Furthermore, there is insufficient evidence that defendant failed to accomodate plaintiff's heart condition or subjected plaintiff to an adverse employment action due to the same. Plaintiff's evidence on this issue is insufficient to create a triable issue of fact. As for the discrimination claim, defendant shifted the burden of production of evidence by presenting evidence that plaintff was not subjected to an adverse employment action because plaintff was told she had the option of complying with terms of the action plans, but instead chose to quit. The plan itself and the poor performance review are not adverse employment actions. Yanowitz v. L'Oreal (2005) 36 Cal 4th 1028. While plaintiff does create a triable issue of fact as to whether she was forced out as opposed to voluntarily quitting, she has insufficent evidence that this was connected to her claimed disabilites and plaintiff does not create a triable issue of material fact as to whether defendant's legitimate business reasons (plaintiff's poor performance) were pretextual. Plaintiff relies on temporal proximity from when she fainted to when she was placed on the action plan. This, standing alone, is not enough to rebut an employer's evidence of a legitimate business purpose. Arteaga v. Brink's, Inc. (2008) 163 C.A. 4th 327. Accordingly, the wrongful discharge cause of action fails for the same reason the discrimination cause of action fails. Because there i

rulings.Case Title: HUGH ANTHONY KING VS. MATTHEW WARD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Deft Luis Alvarez To Provide Furtehr Responses To Special Interrogatories, Form Interrogatories, And Requests For Admissions, And To Provide Responses To Request For Production, And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 28, 2011, line 1. PLAINTIFF HUGH KING'S Motion To Compel Defendant Luis Alvarez To Provide Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Form Interrogatories, And Requests For Admissions and production of documents is granted. Defendant must provide verified supplemental respnses by May 16, 2011. Motion for sanctions granted. Defendant's counsel must remit $1,500 to plaintiff's counsel by May 31, 2011. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JANINE BOCCIARDI VS. APPEARANCE ENHANCEMENT CLINIC, A MEDICAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 28, 2011, line 12. DEFENDANT FRED SUESS, M.D.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. A complaint can contain inconsistent or alternative theories of liability that arise out of the same set of facts. Crowley v. Katleman (1994) 8 Cal 4th 666, 690-91. Further, plaintiff has pled facts with sufficient specificity to support her causes of action. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: ANNE W. THAYER VS. KABATECK BROWN KELLNER LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 28, 2011, line 6. DEFENDANTS KABATECK BROWN KELLNER LLP, RICHARD KELLNER'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE is denied. Because the prayers for relief regarding the settlement are moot, there is no basis to transfer venue to L.A. All the remaining allegations and prayers go to malpractice. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: ANNE W. THAYER VS. KABATECK BROWN KELLNER LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 28, 2011, line 6. DEFENDANTS KABATECK BROWN KELLNER LLP, RICHARD KELLNER'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is denied without prejudice. While most of defendant's arguments are well taken, it is clear that the timing of this motion deprives plaintiff of an opportunity to amend her complaint per the Court's previous rulings on the defendant's demurrer. Plaintiff's request for attorneys fees is denied as the anti-SLAPP motion is not "frivolous". =(302/LMG)
